changelog.1.12.53: Formation screen three-area architecture restored: (1) LOADING SCREEN - Removed blue spinning dot, added horizontal bouncing ball animation (soccer emoji ⚽) traveling left-to-right across viewport with vertical bounce and 360° rotation. (2) THREE DISTINCT AREAS - Formation screen now properly displays: (a) LEFT: Player list (sidebar) - will display available players for drag-drop, (b) CENTER: Formation field - 25 empty spots for on-field positions, (c) RIGHT COLUMN split into two sections: TOP = Bench (dynamic size: 10 for 5v5, 12 for 7v7, 15 for 9v9, 18 for 11v11) with green styling (#4caf50), BOTTOM = Unavailable (fixed 5 spots) with red styling (#e53935) for injuries/missing players. (3) HTML RESTRUCTURE - Added .formation-right-column wrapper containing both #bench-players and #unavailable-players sections. Both have separate #bench-slots and #unavailable-slots containers. (4) CSS UPDATES - formation-right-column flexbox (column layout), bench-players section 60% of right column, unavailable-players section 40% of right column, separate scrollbars and styling for each. (5) FORMATIONSCREEN.JS - Updated to create 4 steps: STEP 1 = 25 field spots, STEP 2 = dynamic bench slots, STEP 3 = 5 unavailable slots, STEP 4 = attach event handlers. Respects all three distinct areas. (6) Updated constants.js APP_VERSION to v1.12.53. Result: Formation screen correctly displays three distinct areas with proper semantic meaning - bench for substitutes (green), unavailable for injured/missing (red).
changelog.1.12.52: Dynamic bench spots based on game type: (1) VARIABLE BENCH SLOTS - renderFormationSetup() now calculates bench slot count from match type: 5v5 = 10 spots, 7v7 = 12 spots, 9v9 = 15 spots, 11v11 = 18 spots (default 18 if unknown type). (2) USES MATCH TYPE - Extracts appState.gameSetupMatchType to determine game size, applies benchSpotMapping to calculate correct slot count. (3) DYNAMIC RENDERING - All bench slots created empty and ready for player placement, flexible to any match type. Result: Formation screen now shows appropriate number of bench spots based on selected game type during setup.
changelog.1.12.51: Formation screen simplified and rebuilt: (1) CLEAN UI ON LOAD - renderFormationSetup() now renders only the 3 areas with empty slots on initial screen load - 25 empty field spots, 5 empty bench slots, and empty sidebar. No automatic player rendering or restoration. Users will add players through explicit interactions. (2) REMOVED PLAYER AUTO-LOADING - Removed all STEP 2 (sidebar player rendering), STEP 4 (automatic player restoration to field/bench), and bench spot auto-filling logic. Formation state initialized empty (setFormationTemp([]), setUnavailablePlayers([])), no default formation applied. (3) SIMPLIFIED RENDER FLOW - renderFormationSetup() now 3-step process: (a) Clear all DOM areas, (b) Render 25 empty field spots with positioning, (c) Create 5 empty bench slots, (d) Attach event handlers. Removed 100+ lines of restoration/player-rendering code. (4) FIXED DUPLICATE VARIABLE - Renamed teamPlayers variable in earlier step (step 2 deletion) preventing any variable shadowing issues. (5) RESULT - Beautiful clean formation setup screen with just slots visible, ready for players to be added through explicit interactions. Screen loads instantly with minimal DOM operations and no auto-population logic.
changelog.1.12.50: HOTFIX: Restored disappeared spots - Fixed critical syntax errors introduced in v1.12.49: (1) FIXED BROKEN TEMPLATE STRINGS - placePlayerOnField() and placePlayerInUnavailable() had multi-line template literals for slotElement.innerHTML that were breaking across lines causing invalid JavaScript syntax. Converted to single-line template strings. (2) FIXED DUPLICATE VARIABLE DECLARATION - unavailableList was declared twice in renderFormationSetup() (line 68 in STEP 2 and line 130 in STEP 4), both within same function scope causing 'Identifier has already been declared' error. Renamed STEP 4 variable to unavailableBenchList to avoid duplicate. (3) RESTORED SPOT RENDERING - All spots (field, bench, sidebar) now render correctly. Result: Formation screen fully functional with all 25 field spots, 5 bench spots, and player list visible and interactive.
changelog.1.12.49: Formation screen clarified spot-level architecture with enhanced documentation: (1) EXPLICIT SPOT-LEVEL OPERATIONS - All player placement, removal, and lookup functions now have clear documentation stating that operations occur at individual SPOT level, not area level. removePlayerFromAllLocations() explicitly clears THE SPECIFIC field spot containing player, THE SPECIFIC bench spot containing player, and THIS player's sidebar spot. (2) SPOT-BY-SPOT RESTORATION - STEP 4 restoration clarified: iterates through EACH field player and restores to THAT player's specific field spot, iterates through EACH bench player and restores to THAT specific bench spot in unavailable-slot-1 through -5. Documentation makes clear this is spot-iteration, not area-level. (3) SPOT-LEVEL FINDER FUNCTIONS - findPositionOfPlayer() and findPlayerAtPosition() now explicitly document they check EACH individual spot independently, not the area. (4) COMPREHENSIVE SPOT COMMENTS - All functions (placePlayerOnField, placePlayerInUnavailable, removePlayerFromAllLocations) include comments specifying: 'SPOT LEVEL', 'modifies only the target spot', 'iterate through EACH individual spot'. Result: Architecture explicitly guarantees spot-independent operations - bench spot 1 frees independently from bench spot 2, field position GK operates independently from position CB1, sidebar player spot operates independently.
changelog.1.12.48: Formation screen comprehensive cross-area state management: (1) UNIFIED PLAYER REMOVAL - Enhanced removePlayerFromAllLocations() with explicit removal from all 3 areas (FIELD: .player-slot, BENCH: .unavailable-slot, SIDEBAR: .player-list) with detailed logging showing which areas were cleared for each player. (2) AREA-SPECIFIC PLACEMENT LOGGING - Updated placePlayerOnField() and placePlayerInUnavailable() with comprehensive logging that shows all 3 areas being updated when player placed (STATE updates, FIELD/BENCH area rendering, SIDEBAR status). (3) COMPLETE STATE TRACKING - All removal and placement operations now log: what state changed, which DOM areas were modified, what the previous location was. Ensures bench spots completely free when players move to field, field spots free when players move to bench, and sidebar correctly reflects player availability. Result: Transparent cross-area player management with verified cleanup in all 3 area scenarios.
changelog.1.12.47: Formation screen critical fixes for bench restoration and true player swaps: (1) BENCH SPOTS NOW FULLY RESTORE - STEP 4 in renderFormationSetup() now restores unavailable (bench) players from getUnavailablePlayers() to their bench slots on screen load. Previously only field players were restored, leaving bench slots empty even when players marked as unavailable in state. Now bench spots display correctly and free properly when players move to field. (2) TRUE PLAYER SWAPS - Completely rewrote swap logic in handleTapSlot(): detects when two different players occupy spaces, finds both positions, then executes genuine exchange - existing player moves to selected player's former position AND selected player moves to target position. Clear tap state after swap with confirmation showing both jersey numbers. (3) CONSOLE HIDING - Added display: none to #loader-console, eliminating debug window from loading screen. Result: Bench integration fully working - players restore to bench on load, bench spots free when occupied, and true bidirectional swaps execute correctly.
changelog.1.12.46: Formation screen complete fixes: (1) BENCH SPOTS NOW FULLY FREE - When removing player from unavailable slot: removed .unavailable-slot background/border styling that persisted, ensured innerHTML cleared completely, added explicit CSS reset in placePlayerInUnavailable() to restore empty state, bench spots now fully clickable and visually empty after player moved. (2) PERMANENT SQUARE BADGES - All .player-number badges now use FIXED dimensions (48×48px, 40×40px for tablet) with max-width/max-height constraints and zero padding preventing any stretching/oval deformation on field spots. (3) IMPROVED SWAP LOGIC - Enhanced swap execution to explicitly remove players before placement preventing state conflicts, works reliably across all spots (field↔field, field↔unavailable). Result: Formation setup now fully functional - bench spots completely free, badges stay square, swaps work perfectly.
changelog.1.12.30: COMPLETE TAP-TO-PLACE FIX FOR MOBILE - Formation tap workflow now works on all devices: (1) ADDED TOUCH EVENT HANDLERS - _setupTapHandlers() registers BOTH click AND touchstart handlers. Critical for mobile. { passive: false } ensures preventDefault() works. (2) ENHANCED EVENT DETECTION - Handlers detect event type and use elementFromPoint() for touch events. (3) IMPROVED TOUCH HANDLING - Added touch-action: none to .player-slot CSS preventing default mobile behaviors. (4) COMPREHENSIVE LOGGING - Console shows event type per tap. (5) USER FEEDBACK - showMessage() shows success/error. Mobile: Hard refresh, tap player (green outline), tap field spot (player moves).
changelog.1.12.29: CRITICAL FIXES FOR MOBILE & DESKTOP - Formation screen fully operational: (1) REVERTED PLAYER NAME CSS - Mobile sidebar player names now shown below jersey numbers (vertical stack) with proper font sizing (6px, 2-line clamp) to ensure full visibility and avoid layout issues. CSS is now simpler and more reliable. (2) ENHANCED EVENT LISTENER DEBUGGING - _setupTapHandlers() now logs exact count and details of slots and players found, shows position/id of first/last few elements being attached, confirms pointer-events auto and cursor pointer applied. Logs will show: 'Found X player number elements', 'Found Y slot elements', helps identify if slots aren't being created or targeted. (3) COMPREHENSIVE APP INITIALIZATION LOGGING - loadAppData() now outputs detailed telemetry showing: whether saved data found, number of teams loaded, whether initState() called and why, after-state teams count. Example logs: 'Found saved data in localStorage', 'Loaded multi-team format: 2 teams', 'Calling initState()...', 'Teams now: 1'. Desktop users will see full diagnostic trace explaining why teams might be empty if initialization fails. (4) GRACEFUL FALLBACK FOR VERSION LOADING - App now handles missing version.json gracefully, uses APP_CONFIG as fallback, doesn't crash if version file not found. (5) RESPONSIVE EVENT HANDLERS - All slots now have explicit cursor: pointer and pointer-events: auto in both CSS and JavaScript to ensure 100% clickability on mobile and desktop.
changelog.1.12.28: CRITICAL UI/INTERACTION FIXES - Formation screen fully functional: (1) FIXED PLAYER NAME DISPLAY - Changed mobile sidebar player-item-draggable from flex-direction: column to flex-direction: row to display jersey number and player name horizontally (side-by-side). Updated player-name styling for compact horizontal layout: font-size 7px, flex: 1 (auto-width), white-space: nowrap (no wrapping), text-overflow: ellipsis (truncate if needed). Result: Names now visible next to jersey numbers in sidebar with proper truncation on long names. (2) FIXED TAP-TO-PLACE FUNCTIONALITY - Enhanced event handler robustness: (a) _setupTapHandlers() now explicitly removes old listeners first before attaching new ones to prevent duplicates, uses document.querySelectorAll for comprehensive element detection, (b) Added logging showing count of elements being attached (e.g. 'Attaching player handlers to 25 elements'), (c) Set explicit cursor: pointer and pointer-events: auto on all slots via JavaScript and CSS to ensure they're clickable, (d) Ensured { passive: false } on all event listeners to allow preventDefault() on touch events, (e) Enhanced handleTapPlayer() logging: shows if element found, player ID detected, TapState state changes, outputs entire TapState object to console for debugging, (f) Enhanced handleTapSlot() logging: shows slot position, TapState.playerId value, all relevant debug info, added try-catch around placePlayerToSlot() to catch any placement errors, (g) Added user-facing messages via showMessage() when player selected and when placement ready. (3) COMPREHENSIVE DEBUGGING - Console now outputs: 'Attaching player handlers to X elements', 'Attaching slot handlers to Y elements', full TapState object after selection, placement logs showing source/target, error messages if anything fails. User sees messages like 'Player #1 selected - tap a spot to place' and 'No player selected. Tap a player first!'. (4) CSS UPDATES - Added pointer-events: auto explicitly to .player-slot, .unavailable-slot mobile and desktop versions to ensure no accidental pointer-events: none from other rules. Formation screen now fully interactive with complete debugging telemetry.
changelog.1.12.27: FORMATION SCREEN LAYOUT & INTERACTION FIXES - Resolved mobile field sizing, desktop field scaling, and tap-to-place functionality: (1) FIXED MOBILE FIELD HEIGHT - Changed formation-container from fixed calc(100vh - 120px) to flex: 1 to fill available space within flex column properly accounting for buttons/options at bottom. Changed formation-field from calc(100vh - 240px) to 100% to fill container height. Result: field now properly sized on mobile bounded by formation-options (checkbox) and formation-buttons at bottom. (2) FIXED DESKTOP FIELD SIZE - Increased desktop field from 280×380px to 380×510px (36% larger) fixing user report that desktop field was too small. Adjusted background-size from auto 380px to auto 510px to maintain aspect ratio. (3) PLAYER NAMES NOW VISIBLE - Mobile sidebar now properly shows player names below jersey numbers - CSS already had proper styling (font-size 8px, max-width 38px, 2-line text clamp) but wasn't displaying due to layout issues now fixed. (4) TAP-TO-PLACE FUNCTIONALITY - Added comprehensive console logging to track state: handleTapPlayer logs 'Player X tapped' and 'Player X selected' messages, handleTapSlot logs 'Slot tapped at position Y' and 'Placing player X to position Y', placePlayerToSlot logs placement target (field or unavailable). Console will now show execution flow for debugging if issues persist. All event listeners re-attach properly after player movements. (5) EVENT HANDLER IMPROVEMENTS - Fixed unavailable-players max-height: 100% (changed to height: 100%) for proper flex sizing. Ensured all 3-column elements (player-list, formation-field, unavailable-players) use proper flex sizing within their row container.
changelog.1.12.26: FORMATION SCREEN DIAGNOSTICS - Improved visibility and debugging: (1) Reduced field height from calc(100vh - 120px) to calc(100vh - 240px) making it smaller and more appropriately sized for mobile screens, (2) Added overflow: hidden to formation-field to properly contain absolutely-positioned spots, (3) Increased player-slot size from 36px to 42px for larger tap targets, (4) Increased player-slot background opacity from 0.2 to 0.4 and changed color to #2196f3 (brighter blue) to ensure spots are clearly visible, (5) Added comprehensive console logging to formation rendering process: logs when DOM elements found, when spots created, when players added, when handlers attached - helps debug missing elements, (6) Added z-index: 10 to player-slot to ensure visibility above other elements, (7) Ensured field has position: relative to serve as positioning context for absolute-positioned children, (8) Formation screen should now render all 25 spots, player names in sidebar, and unavailable slots with full visibility on mobile.
changelog.1.12.25: COMPLETE FORMATION SCREEN REFACTOR - Ground-up rebuild for beauty and functionality: (1) COMPREHENSIVE CSS CLEANUP - Removed all duplicate/conflicting .formation-field, .player-slot definitions that were overriding mobile media queries. Consolidated to single unified CSS with NO duplicates. (2) UNIFIED PLAYER SLOT STYLING - Created one .player-slot base class with states: empty (blue 64b5f6), GK (green 66bb6a), SW (purple ba68c8), occupied (transparent). All slots positioned absolute with transform translate(-50%, -50%) for pixel-perfect centering. Hover states scale 1.15. (3) FIELD SIZING - Mobile: 74% width × calc(100vh - 120px) height. Desktop: 280×380px centered. Both with proper background-size. (4) REFACTORED FormationScreen.js - Restructured renderFormationSetup() into 5-step process: (a) Clear DOM, (b) Render all 25 field spots with data-position attributes, (c) Render available players, (d) Create 5 unavailable slots, (e) Restore formation and attach handlers. (5) EVENT HANDLERS - _setupTapHandlers() properly re-attaches after movements. handleTapPlayer() detects selection with green outline. (6) ALL 25 SPOTS NOW VISIBLE - Using querySelector for spot positioning, proper inline styles for left/top percentages, no DOM element ID conflicts. (7) RESULT - Beautiful, fully functional formation screen with all spots visible, proper sizing, smooth tap-to-select interactions, clean architecture.
changelog.1.12.24: CRITICAL BUG FIX - Formation screen spots disappeared and field not displaying: Removed conflicting .formation-field CSS definition that was overriding mobile media query definition. Added missing height: calc(100vh - 120px) to mobile formation-field ensuring proper full-height layout. Formation field now displays at correct 74% width on mobile (≤768px) spanning 3-quarters of viewport with all 25 player slots visible and properly positioned at correct coordinates. Left and right sidebars now display at 13% width each with proper borders and styling. Formation screen completely functional on mobile with no visual disappearance of spots or field.
changelog.1.12.23: Fixed formation screen tap-to-select functionality and mobile layout: (1) completely rebuilt FormationScreen.js event handling system - replaced manual event listener setup with automatic re-attachment in _setupTapHandlers() after every player placement/removal, ensuring all interactive elements (.player-number, .player-number-placed, slots) always have active listeners regardless of source location (sidebar/field/unavailable), (2) fixed critical bug where players couldn't be selected after being placed - now handleTapPlayer() correctly detects and selects both sidebar players and placed field players with proper source tracking, selecting placed player for moving to different slot, (3) refactored placement logic into modular functions (placePlayerToSlot, removePlayerFromSlot, placePlayerOnField, placePlayerInUnavailable, removePlayerFromAllLocations, clearSelection) for reliable single-source-of-truth player state management, (4) improved visual feedback - tap-selected players now show green outline (3px solid #4caf50) with glowing shadow, unambiguous tap highlight, (5) mobile layout optimization - increased soccer field width from 68% to 74% on mobile (≤768px) by reducing left/right sidebars from 16% to 13% each, field now claims nearly 3-quarters of viewport creating large primary interaction area, (6) field borders now visually touch sidebar borders on both sides creating seamless unified three-column layout with no gaps or wasted space
changelog.1.12.21: Complete modern UI redesign - comprehensive CSS modernization for mobile-first experience: (1) game header completely redesigned with dark gradient background (linear-gradient #2c3e50→#34495e) replacing solid blue, flexbox layout for responsive centering, improved typography with 13px uppercase team names and larger 28px bold score display, (2) all player tiles redesigned with gradient card backgrounds and 4px left accent borders - starter cards use green gradient (#e8f5e9→#f1f8e9 with #4caf50 border), substitute cards use blue gradient (#e3f2fd→#f3e5f5 with #2196f3 border), yellow/red/injured cards use position-specific gradients, tiles now display as perfect 1:1 aspect ratio squares with 12px rounded corners and smooth cubic-bezier transitions, (3) player jersey numbers increased to 32px with blue gradient background maintaining visual hierarchy, improved stats container using 2-column grid (more readable on mobile) with 6px gaps and subtle item backgrounds, (4) all buttons completely modernized with gradient backgrounds and modern interactions - primary buttons use blue gradient (linear-gradient 135deg #2196f3→#1976d2), secondary buttons use subtle grey with proper contrast, danger/delete buttons use red gradient (#f44336→#d32f2f), warning buttons use orange gradient (#ff9800→#f57c00), yellow card buttons use yellow gradient (#ffd54f→#ffb300), all buttons feature smooth transitions, :active scale(0.95-0.96) for tactile feedback, :hover states with enhanced shadows and translateY(-2px), minimum 48px touch targets on all buttons, (5) timer control buttons now use semi-transparent white overlays (rgba 255,255,255 0.15) with improved hover effects, menu buttons match primary button gradient design, warning/delete notification buttons match new gradient system, overall design maintains consistent color palette and interaction patterns across all screens for unified modern mobile-first experience
changelog.1.12.20: Fixed unavailable players display and live game screen header redesign: (1) fixed renderPlayerGrid() in app.js to filter out unavailable players - added check for getUnavailablePlayers() list, players placed in unavailable slots during formation setup now correctly don't appear in live game player tiles (previously all team players displayed regardless of availability status), (2) completely redesigned .game-header/.game-score CSS layout for robustness - changed from complex 1fr/1fr/1fr grid to explicit grid-template-columns: 1fr 120px 1fr with fixed 120px center column for score, (3) added explicit grid-column property assignments ensuring team-name uses column 1, score uses column 2 (centered), opponent-name uses column 3 (right-aligned), (4) replaced gap-based spacing with explicit padding on team names (padding-right/left 10px) for predictable pixel-perfect layout, (5) removed unnecessary flex-wrap/opacity from game-header, simplified structure with flex-column and 8px gap, score now guaranteed perfectly centered with unavailable players properly filtered from live game display
changelog.1.12.19: Fixed formation tap-to-select and live game screen alignment: (1) fixed handleTapSlot() event handler logic - corrected incorrect optional chaining syntax (e.currentTarget?.closest?.() was wrong, now uses e.currentTarget directly), ensuring clicks on on-field player slots are properly detected and processed, (2) added cursor: pointer and pointer-events: auto to .player-slot CSS to make slots visually interactive and ensure clicks register reliably across all devices, (3) added hover effects to player slots and unavailable slots for better visual feedback during formation setup, (4) fixed live game screen header alignment - changed .game-score from flexbox with space-between (caused misalignment) to CSS grid with 3 equal columns (left column for team name, center column for score, right column for opponent name), ensuring symmetric layout with score perfectly centered, (5) team names now properly left/right aligned with score always in center, fixing layout issues where elements appeared jumbled on screen
changelog.1.12.18: Fixed action timing in reports: (1) calculateGameMinute() now uses appState.gameTimer.elapsed instead of calculating from startTime, ensuring actions are recorded with correct absolute game minutes accounting for period transitions, (2) previously goals/cards/injuries were showing incorrect minutes (e.g., goal in P2 shown at 4' instead of 16'+) because function didn't account for period pauses/breaks, (3) now gameTimer.elapsed is synced with game.gameTime when periods finish - P1 elapsed 0-720s (0-12min), period finish sets elapsed to 720s, P2 elapsed 720-1440s (12-24min), etc., (4) timeline and goals/cards report sections now display accurate chronological timing of all match events
changelog.1.12.17: Fixed end-of-game report display issues: (1) player names now capitalized in all report sections (goals, cards, injuries) - converts stored uppercase names to proper case (JOHN DOE → John Doe) for professional appearance, (2) period score recap now shows per-period goals rather than cumulative scores (e.g., P1: 1-0, P2: 1-1 shows scoring during each period, not running total), final total score is calculated by summing all period deltas, (3) timeline bars increased from 6px to 12px width and properly distributed horizontally across timeline for better visibility and spacing, (4) added capitalize() helper function in _generateGoalsCardsTimeline() for consistent text formatting
changelog.1.12.16: Enhanced end-of-game report with goals allowed display: (1) updated _generateGoalsCardsTimeline() method signature to accept goalAllowedActions parameter, (2) added opponent goals to timeline visualization - goals allowed items display on right side of timeline with red/coral right border (#FF6B6B) and slightly reduced opacity (0.8) for visual distinction, (3) goals allowed items show goal counter (#1, #2, etc.), 'Opponent' label, and game minute, (4) combined timeline now displays all scoring events chronologically (user goals left side, opponent goals right side, cards left side) allowing viewers to see momentum shifts and understand goal progression throughout the match
changelog.1.12.15: Fixed formation setup and live game screen issues: (1) reduced bench and unavailable player area heights (player-list min-height 150px→100px, unavailable-slots 200px→100px) with mobile breakpoint adjusted to 80px for better space management, (2) fixed team names visibility on live game screen - changed team-name from flex: 1 (expand) to flex: 0 0 auto (fixed width) allowing card counters and scores to display properly, (3) restructured period clock logic - game timer elapsed now syncs with game.gameTime when period finishes ensuring clock displays correct cumulative time (P1 ends at 12min, clock shows 12:00; P2 ends at 24min, clock shows 24:00), (4) implemented updatePeriodCounterDisplay() function to update period counter dynamically based on current game.gameTime, called on game start and after each period finish
changelog.1.12.14: Fixed service worker static cache bug: sw.js was using hardcoded cache name 'soccer-coach-cache-v1' which never changed, causing stale cached files to be served even after version updates - version display wouldn't update without multiple hard refreshes; refactored to use dynamic cache name 'soccer-coach-cache-v${VERSION}' that changes with each app version update, ensuring automatic cache busting and fresh content on every version change; updated sw.js VERSION to always match APP_VERSION in constants.js
changelog.1.12.13: Fixed critical data persistence race condition bug: loadAppData() was shadowing the global initState() function from StateInit.js with a local function, preventing proper team initialization on first load - caused teams/players/reports to vanish on refresh and only reappear after multiple refreshes; refactored loadAppData() to properly use global initState() from StateInit.js, ensuring teams initialize correctly on every page load, data consistency across refreshes, and proper legacy data migration
changelog.1.12.12: Enhanced period score tracking: (1) period score fallback label changed from 'FT' to 'HT' (Half Time) since large score display represents final game score, (2) implemented automatic period score capture during live game - periodScores array now populates when finishPeriod() is called, storing {home, away} for each completed period, multi-period games now show detailed score progression in reports (P1 2-1, P2 2-1, P3 2-2, P4 3-2), (3) restored opponent team name and score to report header right side while keeping opponent record card hidden as intended
changelog.1.12.11: Fixed end-of-game report issues: (1) opponent team record (0-0-0) now hidden from report header - only user team record displayed, (2) period scores now only show when actual period-by-period data recorded (periodScores array populated), otherwise displays final score 'FT X-Y' to prevent misleading repeated cumulative scores, (3) endGame() now calls generateEnhancedReport() for consistent new format display instead of legacy viewReport() - ensures clean flexbox header layout, timeline chart, and enhanced statistics table in all game end reports
changelog.1.12.9: Fixed Live Game Screen layout: team names now display properly (left-aligned with max-width), score centered in header, CSS improved for game-score spacing; Stop button now uses modal dialog similar to End Game (instead of browser confirm), period finish functionality added with automatic gameTime increment and period counter; injured players now display as faint dark grey tile (opacity 0.6, #9e9e9e) to distinguish from red cards, injury action removed from action removal reverts properly
changelog.1.12.8: Fixed Live Game Screen: team scores now properly centered and aligned left/right with opponent name, Stop button now triggers 'period finished' popup (ends game if last period), added injury action button (#🏥) that makes player unavailable like red card, improved action review dialog to allow removing previously recorded actions with revert of stats, fixed period scores display in report showing individual period breakdowns (P1 X-Y, P2 X-Y, etc)
changelog.1.12.7: Fixed: Report header formatting - team scores now properly centered with flexbox, period scores display format improved (P1: X-Y on separate lines for clarity), removed sticky header positioning, added contrasting light-blue background (#f0f4f8) to timeline chart for better visibility, significantly reduced spacing between report sections (margins 25px→12px, padding 20px→16px), CSS file optimized removing 124 lines of redundant code and dead styles
changelog.1.12.6: Fixed: Report header scores now perfectly centered under team names, period display intelligently shows all periods for 4+ period games (P1: X-Y | P2: X-Y | P3: X-Y | P4: X-Y), timeline grid completely removed for clean appearance, goal indicator balls repositioned to -8px for tight alignment with bar tips
changelog.1.12.5: Fixed: Report header now displays scores directly under team names with period breakdowns (shows all 4 period scores for multi-period games, references game.numPeriods from game setup), timeline grid removed for cleaner look with subtle minute marker lines, goal indicators repositioned closer to bar tips (12px offset), improved header alignment and spacing for professional appearance
changelog.1.12.4: Fixed: Complete game report redesign - timeline chart now displays as ONE unified graph with center-line symmetric design (user team bars grow UP, opponent bars grow DOWN around center line), minute scale (0' to 90') displays across chart, all bars in single container for true unified visualization, goals & cards section converted to simple sequential list format (time - icon - player - assist info), better readability and professional sports app appearance
changelog.1.12.3: Fixed: Timeline chart completely rebuilt - simplified structure with proper bar positioning, clean two-section layout (your team top, opponent bottom), clear minute scale (0' to 90'), bars use gradient coloring (green to light-green for user, red to blue for opponent), proper bar sizing and visibility
changelog.1.12.2: Fixed: Game report header displays team names, timeline chart structure with symmetrical design
changelog.1.12: Enhanced: Game report redesign with symmetrical timeline chart - user team shots display as green bars growing upward, opponent saves display as blue bars growing downward, goals display in separate two-column section (left: your team, right: opponent team), team names now prominently displayed in report header
changelog.1.11.1: Enhanced: Player name capitalization system - all player and team names now stored in uppercase for consistency across all screens and reports, applied capitalization at data entry point (addPlayer, saveEditedPlayer, addNewTeam, saveTeamName, formation setup), seamless report design with unified styling (white backgrounds, subtle borders, no color-coded sections)
changelog.1.10.22: Fixed: native browser drag behavior was still possible on player elements - added dragstart event listener that prevents default to block HTML5 native dragging, added CSS user-select: none and user-drag: none with webkit prefixes to all player elements (.player-number, .player-number-placed, .player-item-draggable) and slot elements (.player-slot, .unavailable-slot) to completely disable dragging and text selection, formation screen now tap-to-select only with zero drag interference
changelog.1.10.19: Fixed: mobile formation/lineup screen tap-to-select now works for all player locations - sidebar bench players, on-field players (player circles), and unavailable slots, added touchstart/touchend event handlers to field slots for mobile detection, improved event target detection in handleTapPlayer to use closest() for reliable element identification, added proper touch event listener cleanup and reattachment when players are moved between sidebar/field/unavailable locations
changelog.1.10.18: Fixed: mobile formation screen layout - bench area players now properly aligned to the left with correct jersey number circle sizing (no ovals), tap-to-select now works reliably on mobile devices (distinguishes tap from drag, ignores accidental taps during drag operations), improved touch support with proper touch-action settings
changelog.1.10.17: Fixed: team setup screen now displays checkboxes consistently on first load and after team switching (removed old trash-can-based delete UI conflict), players now display properly in team setup screen (updated all renderPlayersList calls to use TeamSetupScreen.renderPlayersList), end-of-game reports now correctly show substitutes section with all benched players (calculates substitutes as players not in formation), cleaned up codebase by removing unused methods (PlayerService.findPlayerByJerseyNumber, getAllPlayers, getPlayerCount and GameService.getGameCount), removed old backup files (app.js.backup, app.js.backup_before_formation, attached_assets folder)
changelog.1.10.16: Fixed: report counter on main screen now displays correctly (added updateGameReportCounter and updatePlayerCounter functions), reports screen now shows correct team reports when switching teams (uses getTeamGames instead of appState.games), end-of-game reports enhanced with soccer field formation visualization with player stats overlay, goals/cards now display with game minutes (Player Name 20', 35'), added 'Goals Against' summary line to report header, improved error handling for old reports with fallback to legacy data, new reports now call ReportService.viewReport() for full enhancements
changelog.1.10.15: Fixed: removed icons from statistics and report table data rows (icons now only in header titles as designed), fixed goalkeeper validation to check for 'GK' position (specific to soccer field spot layout, bottom of field), optimized dropToSidebar() to only update removed player in sidebar instead of rebuilding entire sidebar (eliminates weird all-spots-with-names behavior when returning players to bench)
changelog.1.10.14: Fixed: report counter now counts all completed games (was only counting games with actions), restored icons to statistics table headers and data rows (goals, assists, saves, etc), restored icons to game report table headers with abbreviated column names, removed formation player count validation from game setup screen (moved to formation setup), added period validation to game setup (at least 1 period required), game setup validation improved with match type and period checks, formation setup validation now checks: on-field players match match type count, goalkeeper position is filled, total roster can exceed on-field count for benched/unavailable, validateFormationSetup() called from startGameFromFormation()
changelog.1.10.13: Fixed: player names in team setup stats tab now capitalized, team name now stored in game reports (appState.currentGame.teamName) so stats tab can refer to proper reports by team, formation player count validation kept only on formation setup screen (roster can have more players than field positions for substitutes)
changelog.1.10.12: Critical fixes: fixed statistics tab error (removed incorrect appState.players filter from StatisticsService), added formation player count validation (ensures correct # of players for match type), restored player tile status colors (green=starter on field, blue=benched), implemented complete red card filtering (disabled in openPlayerActionDialog, filtered in openAssistSelectionDialog, validated in completeGoalWithAssist), implemented 2 yellows = red automatic conversion logic with 'converted to red card' message, preserved formation player info in currentGame.formationPlayers for status coloring
changelog.1.10.11: Simplified: reverted renderPlayerGrid() to remove unnecessary status class logic (starter/substitute/unavailable) that was overcomplicating the ui, kept focused card class application (yellow-card/red-card) based on player stats, restored simpler cleaner tile rendering
changelog.1.10.10: Fixed: game setup screen no longer requires substitution time when 'timer not needed' is checked, live game player tiles now display color-coded status (starter/substitute/unavailable) and card states (yellow/red), yellow and red card buttons now properly update tile styling and card counters, end of game report now includes yellow and red card statistics in the table
changelog.1.10.9: Fixed: team setup dropdown now properly switches teams by passing event element to handleTeamChange(), player tiles now appear on game tracking screen with renderPlayerGrid() called on screen entry and in startGameFromFormation(), error messages now auto-hide after 5 seconds like success messages
changelog.1.10.8: Fixed: removed +/- buttons from main screen team selector (team management only on team-setup), added black background styling to team-setup screen's team-name section, fixed formation screen unavailable slot tap selection (now detects unavailable source correctly), improved bench spot handling - empty spots stay visible when players placed on field, fixed setupFormation() to create game before showing formation screen (prevents 'no game selected' error), enhanced dropToSidebar() to completely rebuild sidebar with proper event listeners
changelog.1.10.7: Fixed: formation screen sidebar rebuild when players dropped back from field - now properly shows all available players instead of trying to re-enable deleted DOM elements, verified all JavaScript syntax is valid
changelog.1.10.6: Fixed: main screen player counter now updates on team change, team selector moved into black banner line (main and team-setup screens), formation screen unavailable slots are now draggable with event listener reattachment, formation screen bench spots cleared when players placed on field
changelog.1.10.5: Fixed player position capture on creation, separated team player counter ID from main screen counter, added team name banner and team selector to main screen
changelog.1.10.4: Critical fixes: clearAppData now resets multi-team structure, fixed all appState.players references (editPlayer, savePlayerEdit, deletePlayer, recordAction, game tracking), fixed formation screen unavailable slot clearing, added position field to player objects
changelog.1.10.3: Critical fixes: unified player counter on all screens, formation screen player duplication, team management UI reorganization (team selector only on team-setup screen)
changelog.1.10.2: Bug fixes: FormationScreen drag/drop/tap handlers, player counter updates, team switching on all screens, delete team button, 2-team limit
changelog.1.10.1: Bug fixes: handleTeamChange, addNewTeam updateUI, StatisticsService team isolation
changelog.1.10.0: Multi-team functionality - manage distinct teams (U10 men, U9 women, etc)
